title: Scepter of Ragnos

{{Quote|One was holding some sort of scepter, and it was glowing.|Jaden Korr, describing what the Scepter looked like while Axmis was holding it|<ref name="JK3" />}}
The '''Scepter of Ragnos''' was a powerful [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Sith artifact/Legends|artifact]] and weapon that once belonged to [[Marka Ragnos]], a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] in the ancient [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. The [[Sword/Legends|sword]] had the ability to siphon and store dark side energy, and it could also release the energy of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] on ordinary beings to make them [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. In [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Tavion Axmis]] obtained the weapon from a [[Unidentified collector (Commenor)|collector]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]] and used it to make many members of the [[Disciples of Ragnos]]—a cult that was dedicated to bringing the long-[[Death/Legends|dead]] Ragnos back to life—Force-sensitive. She and the cult were stopped, however, when [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaden Korr]] [[Battle of Korriban (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|destroyed]] the Scepter. Axmis was obliterated along with it when the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of Ragnos, who had taken over her body, was forced to leave her.

==Description==
{{Quote|But the area around the temple felt strange, like its dark side aura was gone.|Kyle Katarn, talking about what it felt like after the Scepter was used|<ref name="JK3" />}}
The metallic [[Sith artifact/Legends|artifact]] known as the [[Scepter/Legends|Scepter]] of Ragnos was created by [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]] of the ancient [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] [[Time/Legends|sometime]] before [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. The weapon consisted of a [[Sword/Legends|sword]] that terminated in a forked crest. Both the handle and blade tip were striated, and the pommel had a double guard, the inside guard shorter than the outside. A fuller ran vertically down the center of the blade. The Scepter crest and handle were [[Color/Legends|slate gray]], while the blade was silver.<ref name="JK3" />

The Scepter had the ability to siphon and store large amounts of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] power from [[Vergence/Legends|areas]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]; when an area was drained, its dark side aura disappeared. The Scepter could fire beams of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] energy or simply unleash a blast of power. The Scepter had the ability to infuse a [[Death/Legends|dead]] [[Zombie/Legends|body]] with enough Force power to reanimate the corpse's cells. It could also use its power to make ordinary beings [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. Hidden inside the Scepter's handle was Ragnos's [[Sith sword]], which could be taken out and wielded separately.<ref name="JK3" />

==History==
{{Quote|A mere Jedi child will not undo my return.|Marka Ragnos's spirit, after being brought out of his tomb by the scepter|audio=29mar002.flac|<ref name="JK3" />}}
[[File:RagnosScepter.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The possessed Tavion Axmis draws the Sith sword from the Scepter.]]
The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Marka Ragnos, who lived during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], created the Scepter of Ragnos. Ragnos died in 5000 BBY, but his Scepter passed into the possession of a [[Unidentified collector (Commenor)|collector]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]] before [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], [[Tavion Axmis]], a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and the leader of the [[Disciples of Ragnos]]—a cult dedicated to reviving the long-dead Ragnos—found the weapon in the possession of the collector and, after killing the being, found a more vital use than combat for the Scepter. She discovered the Scepter's ability to reanimate a corpse's cells, bringing the dead body back to life. Apart from that, small amounts of the stored Force power could be released on ordinary beings in order to grant them Force-sensitivity, sometimes on a scale equal to fully-trained [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. Axmis used that ability to create an [[Army/Legends|army]] of Force-sensitives from the ranks of the Disciples of Ragnos, mainly the [[Sith cultist/Legends|Sith Cultists]]. However, the effect was not permanent; if the Scepter was ever destroyed, the beings would lose their Force-sensitivity and animation.<ref name="JK3" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy}}
Axmis used the Scepter to siphon dark side energy out of several places, including the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. She also used it as a weapon numerous times, such as on the planet [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], where she used it to attack [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knight [[Jaden Korr]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kyle Katarn]] while they were on a [[Mission to Vjun (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|mission]] there. After gaining so much dark side energy, she attempted to resurrect Marka Ragnos in his [[Tomb of Marka Ragnos|tomb]] on the planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Korr arrived on Korriban and [[Battle of Korriban (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|battled]] Axmis, eventually defeating her. However, Axmis then unleashed the Force energy stored in the Scepter onto the [[sarcophagus]] of the ancient Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]], and Ragnos's [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] was summoned. Ragnos's spirit immediately took control of Axmis's body, but Korr [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|dueled]] the possessed Axmis and destroyed the Scepter. When the weapon was destroyed, Ragnos's spirit was drawn back into the sarcophagus, and Axmis died. Also, those who had been infused with the Scepter's power lost their abilities.<ref name="JK3" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Scepter of Ragnos first appeared in the [[Raven Software]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'', which was released on [[September 16]], [[2003]]. It was later mentioned in the [[2008]] compendium ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]]. In the non-[[canon]]ical dark side alternative ending of ''Jedi Academy'', Korr had no intention to destroy the Scepter. After Korr killed Axmis, Jedi Master Kyle Katarn entered the tomb to stop his former apprentice. After a brief battle, Korr fired a blast of energy at Kyle knocking him out, he then proceeded to fire at the roof causing rubble to fall over a knocked out kyle, partially trapping the Jedi Master in the tomb. Korr then blasted his way out of the tomb with the Scepter. After hijacking an [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]], and taking over the [[Imperial Remnant]], Korr kept the Scepter for personal use.
